article_title: OpenTelemetry Profiles Reaches Public Alpha, Establishing Profiling as the Fourth Observability Signal

content_preview.title: OpenTelemetry Profiles Reaches Public Alpha, Establishing Profiling as the Fourth Observability Signal
content_preview.summary: The OpenTelemetry Profiling SIG has promoted its Profiles signal to public alpha, adding vendor-neutral continuous profiling alongside traces, metrics, and logs. Built on Elastic's donated eBPF agent, the new signal supports over a dozen language runtimes with minimal overhead.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The OpenTelemetry Profiling Special Interest Group announced on March 26 that the Profiles signal has [officially entered public alpha](https://opentelemetry.io/blog/2026/profiles-alpha/), marking the first time the project offers a vendor-neutral standard for continuous production profiling. Profiles joins traces, metrics, and logs as the fourth core observability signal within the OpenTelemetry framework, a [CNCF incubating project](https://www.cncf.io/projects/opentelemetry/) wit...
